goal: From the filled world-brief, generate 3 distinct candidate island layouts as preview images for Chris to pick from -- no live-map or engine changes.
completed: Read the filled docs/world-brief.md (linear quest: west spawn -> Evan's Subway-in-a-village shop -> library by the pond, where Ariana the chicken/duck sits and Irene is the boss).

Extended tools/preview_map.py (backward-compatible): --map/--out flags to render any map file to any path, and a new 'L' library symbol (second building, same house sprite -- brief differentiates shop vs library by position). Default run unchanged; regression-checked.

Generated 3 distinct full-island layouts via a scratchpad generator, each 70x45 and validator-clean (0 problems): A Straight Pilgrimage (dead-straight avenue, library as vanishing point), B Winding Approach (S-bend through hidden woods, reveal at pond), C Lakeside Crescent (lake bites east, path hugs shore, library on a point).

Wrote each as docs/world-options/option-{a,b,c}.txt (ASCII source) + .png (render), and docs/world-options/README.md with 2-3 sentences per option on intent + how each applies the 5 design principles differently, plus how to apply the pick.
PROJECT.md: status -> 'Chris picks a layout'; Locations row for docs/world-options/.
Confirmed scripts/island_map.gd is byte-untouched vs origin; gdformat/gdlint green. Committed + pushed (69e96b7). Surfaced the 3 PNGs to Chris via file delivery.
Stopped at exactly 3 candidates as instructed.
discovered: The tileset has no diagonal or 1-wide strip path/water tiles, so 'curves' must be chunky orthogonal L/S bends; the validator (unmapped edge-mask check) enforces this -- it caught a 1-wide water pinch in option B on the first pass.

Coast-wobble occasionally produces 1-wide water nubs/inlets that fail validation; a smooth_water cleanup (flip unmapped-mask water cells to grass, iterate to fixpoint) reliably fixes them without hand-tuning coordinates.

Hardcoding entity coordinates is brittle (option A first put the library inside the pond); snapping each entity to the nearest valid cell (grass-by-water for library/chicken, path for spawn) is far more robust.

The brief needs TWO buildings (shop + library) but the pack has one house sprite -- carried by position + dialogue, not art, exactly as the brief intends. This introduces the 'L' symbol that level.gd/main.gd will need to learn when a layout is applied.
decisions: Three candidate layouts delivered for Chris to choose; the pick is deferred to him (reviewing on PC).
New map symbol 'L' = library (renders the house sprite; validated on grass; 0-or-1 allowed so the current live map still validates).
Candidate ASCII sources (.txt) are committed alongside the .png renders so the chosen one is reproducible -- it drops straight into island_map.gd's MAP const.
preview_map.py is now the reusable 'generate N candidates' tool via --map/--out, not just a single-map renderer.
Live map (island_map.gd) left untouched, per Chris's standing hold until a layout is chosen.
lessons: For headless tile-map design, generate programmatically + validate + render, then iterate on the IMAGE -- three distinct layouts came together fast because the preview tool caught geometry errors (pond-hole, water pinch) before they'd ever reach the engine.
Snap entities to valid terrain instead of hardcoding coordinates; assert-on-place turns a silent bad map into an immediate, located failure.
A fixpoint 'smooth' cleanup is the cheap fix for autotiler-illegal nubs from organic coastlines -- don't hand-fix each flagged cell.
open_questions: Which layout does Chris pick (A straight / B winding / C lakeside)? That choice drives the next build slice.
Applying the pick: drop its .txt into island_map.gd + teach level.gd and main.gd the 'L' library symbol (a second House prop with collision + Irene's eventual placement).
Where exactly does Irene stand relative to the library building, and does she reuse a recolored player sprite (no NPC art yet)?
Still open from before: the 2P camera rule (awaiting Chris's felt playtest), and the duck swap for Ariana (assets/ducky/ still uncommitted with __MACOSX junk).
intent: Stopped after delivering exactly 3 candidates because the next step is Chris's aesthetic pick, which he'll make at his PC -- and the standing hold forbids touching the live map until then. Deliberately did not over-generate or start applying any option.
next_task: Chris reviews docs/world-options/option-{a,b,c}.png and picks one; then a build slice applies that .txt to island_map.gd and adds 'L' library handling to level.gd + main.gd.
dont_retry: Don't design diagonal or 1-wide path/water -- the tileset can't tile it and the validator rejects it; use 2-wide orthogonal bends.
Don't hardcode entity coordinates onto a generated map -- snap to nearest valid terrain and assert.
Don't touch scripts/island_map.gd until Chris picks a layout (standing hold).
